items.title: Build Pi Agent Docker Add-on
items.description: Build a working Docker add-on that installs pi, exposes it via ttyd/tmux in the HA web UI (ingress), persists credentials across restarts, and loads the HA extension with all custom tools.
items.body:
## Architecture

- s6-overlay services: init-pi (oneshot), init-extension (oneshot), ttyd (longrun)
- `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR=/data/pi-agent` for persistent auth.json, settings.json, sessions
- Extension baked into image at `/opt/ha-extension/`, copied to `/homeassistant/.pi/extensions/home-assistant/` at startup
- ttyd → tmux → pi for web terminal via HA ingress
- Python3 included in image

## Key Design Decisions

- `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` env var (not symlinks) for persistent storage — pi reads `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` in `config.js → getAgentDir()`
- `api_keys` config: list of `provider=key` pairs merged into auth.json (preserves OAuth from `/login`)
- `environment` config: arbitrary KEY=VALUE env vars (for AZURE_OPENAI_BASE_URL, AWS_PROFILE, etc.)
- `init: true` for s6-overlay (auto SUPERVISOR_TOKEN injection via `with-contenv`)
- Extension defaults are correct in container — no .env needed: HA_CONFIG_PATH=/homeassistant, HA_URL=http://supervisor/core, HA_TOKEN=SUPERVISOR_TOKEN
- HA ingress handles auth (no ttyd auth needed)
- Docs cache at `/data/ha-docs/` (extension config.ts auto-detects supervisor URL)

## File Structure

```
addon/
├── config.yaml                    # Add-on manifest (ingress, options, schema)
├── build.yaml                     # Base image per arch (ghcr.io/hassio-addons/base:20.0.1)
├── Dockerfile                     # Alpine + nodejs + npm + python3 + ripgrep + tmux + ttyd + pi
├── extensions/home-assistant/     # HA extension source (copied from .pi/extensions/home-assistant/)
├── rootfs/
│   ├── usr/bin/
│   │   └── pi-entrypoint.sh       # Launched by tmux — sources env, cd /homeassistant, exec pi
│   └── etc/s6-overlay/s6-rc.d/
│       ├── init-pi/               # Oneshot: PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR, auth.json, settings.json, env vars, SUPERVISOR_TOKEN
│       ├── init-extension/        # Oneshot: copy extension to /homeassistant/.pi/extensions/
│       ├── ttyd/                  # Longrun: ttyd → tmux → pi-entrypoint.sh
│       └── user/contents.d/      # init-pi, init-extension, ttyd
```

## config.yaml Options

| Option | Type | Default | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|---------|
| `default_provider` | select | `anthropic` | Default AI provider (anthropic/openai/google/openrouter/groq/xai/mistral/cerebras/huggingface/github-copilot/amazon-bedrock/google-vertex/azure-openai-responses) |
| `default_model` | str | `""` | Default model (empty = provider default) |
| `api_keys` | list[password] | `[]` | API keys as `provider=key` pairs, merged into auth.json |
| `environment` | list[str] | `[]` | Extra env vars as `KEY=VALUE` |
| `yolo_mode` | bool | `false` | Auto-approve all tool calls |
| `additional_packages` | list[str] | `[]` | Extra Alpine packages to install |

## Supported Providers (from pi-ai source: env-api-keys.js)

anthropic (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), openai (OPENAI_API_KEY), google (GEMINI_API_KEY), openrouter (OPENROUTER_API_KEY), groq (GROQ_API_KEY), xai (XAI_API_KEY), mistral (MISTRAL_API_KEY), cerebras (CEREBRAS_API_KEY), huggingface (HF_TOKEN), minimax (MINIMAX_API_KEY), zai (ZAI_API_KEY), azure-openai-responses (AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY), github-copilot (GITHUB_TOKEN), kimi-coding (KIMI_API_KEY). Also amazon-bedrock (AWS env vars) and google-vertex (ADC).

## init-pi Script Key Logic

```bash
PI_DIR=/data/pi-agent
mkdir -p "${PI_DIR}/sessions"
printf '%s' "${PI_DIR}" > /var/run/s6/container_environment/PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR

# Merge api_keys config into existing auth.json (preserves OAuth)
# Format: provider=key → {"provider": {"type": "api_key", "key": "..."}}

# Merge settings.json (defaultProvider, defaultModel) without overwriting other fields
# Write environment vars to s6 env dir
# Export SUPERVISOR_TOKEN as HA_TOKEN, set HA_URL and HA_CONFIG_PATH
```

## Reference

- Claude Code addon at `/tmp/hass-claude-code/addon/` as pattern reference for s6 services, ttyd, ingress

relevantFiles.reason: Current run script (to be replaced by s6 services)
Extension entry point to be baked into image
Extension config with HA_CONFIG_PATH, HA_URL, PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR defaults

todos.text: Create Dockerfile (Alpine + nodejs + npm + python3 + ripgrep + tmux + ttyd + pi)
Create build.yaml (base image per arch)
Create config.yaml (ingress, options: default_provider, default_model, api_keys, environment, yolo_mode, additional_packages)
Create init-pi s6 service (PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR, auth.json from api_keys, settings.json, env vars, SUPERVISOR_TOKEN)
Create init-extension s6 service (copy extension to /homeassistant/.pi/extensions/)
Create ttyd s6 longrun service (ttyd → tmux → pi-entrypoint.sh)
Create pi-entrypoint.sh (source env, cd /homeassistant, exec pi)
Create s6 service dependency chain and user/contents.d files
Copy HA extension source into addon/extensions/home-assistant/
Deploy to dev VM and test build
Verify ingress works (sidebar → web terminal → pi)
Verify all ha_* tools work inside container
Verify credentials persist across rebuilds
Verify sessions survive container restart

successCriteria: Add-on builds and starts on dev VM
All ha_* tools functional (entities, automations, graph, etc.)
API keys from config persist in auth.json across rebuilds

items.title: Custom Component — pi_agent Service Registration
items.description: Allow the Pi Agent add-on to register a native HA service (`pi_agent.ask`) that can be called from automations, scripts, and the UI to start a Pi process with a question.
items.body:
## Approach

Self-deploying custom component — the add-on bundles a small Python integration and drops it into `/homeassistant/custom_components/pi_agent/` on startup.

## How It Works

1. Add-on config option: `install_integration: true` (default)
2. Startup copies bundled component to `/homeassistant/custom_components/pi_agent/`
3. Version-stamped deployment (only overwrites when add-on ships newer version)
4. First install triggers HA restart to load the integration
5. Component registers `pi_agent.ask` service
6. Service calls hit HTTP endpoint inside the add-on container
7. Add-on spawns `pi` with the provided question

## Communication

Small HTTP server in the add-on (e.g., Python aiohttp or shell-based). The custom component calls it via `aiohttp.ClientSession`.

## Files to Create

### In the add-on image (`addon/component/pi_agent/`)

- `__init__.py` — ~50 lines, registers `pi_agent.ask` service, calls add-on's HTTP endpoint
- `manifest.json` — integration metadata (name, domain, version, dependencies)
- `services.yaml` — service schema for UI (question field)
- `VERSION` — version stamp for update detection

### Service Schema

```yaml
ask:
  name: Ask Pi Agent
  description: Send a question to the Pi coding agent
  fields:
    question:
      name: Question
      description: The question or task for the Pi agent
      required: true
      selector:
        text:
          multiline: true
```

## Prior Art

- HACS bootstraps itself via similar self-deploy pattern
- Several community add-ons deploy custom components this way

todos.text: Create custom component Python files (__init__.py, manifest.json, services.yaml, VERSION)
Add install_integration config option to add-on
Add startup deployment logic (version-stamped copy to custom_components)
Add HTTP listener in add-on to receive service calls
Spawn pi process from HTTP handler with question
Test service call from automation
Handle uninstall (toggle off removes component)

successCriteria: pi_agent.ask service appears in HA developer tools
Service can be called from automations and scripts
Pi processes question and returns result
Version-stamped: only updates when newer version shipped

items.title: ha_history — Entity State History Tool
items.description: Query historical state changes for entities over a time period. REST API: `GET /api/history/period/<timestamp>`. Actions: get (state history), stats (min/max/mean for sensors).
items.body:
## API

`GET /api/history/period/<timestamp>?filter_entity_id=<id>&end_time=<ts>&minimal_response`

## Parameters

- entity_id (required), start (relative: 1h/24h/7d), end, minimal (default true)

## Output Formatting

Summarize rather than dump raw data:
- State changes: count, first/last change, duration in each state
- Sensors: min/max/mean/current over the period
- Binary sensors: total on-time, number of triggers, last trigger

## Use Cases

- "When was the front door last opened?"
- "What was the temperature overnight?"
- "How many times did motion trigger today?"

todos.text: Add relative time parsing utility to lib/format.ts
Create tools/ha-history.ts with get and stats actions
Implement smart summarization for state changes vs sensor data
Test with real entity history on dev VM

items.title: ha_logbook — Event Logbook Tool
items.description: Query the HA logbook for human-readable activity entries. REST API: `GET /api/logbook/<timestamp>`. Filter by entity, time range, with limit. Shows timeline of what happened.
items.body:
## API

`GET /api/logbook/<timestamp>?entity=<id>&end_time=<ts>`

## Parameters

- entity_id (optional filter), start (relative: 1h/24h/7d), end, limit (default 50)

## Output

Simple timeline format:
```
10:32 AM  motion_sensor_hallway  detected motion
10:33 AM  light.hallway          turned on (by automation: Motion Lights)
10:48 AM  light.hallway          turned off
```

## Use Cases

- "What happened in the house today?"
- "Show all activity for the garage door this week"
- "What triggered automation X?"

Can share relative time parsing utility with ha_history (lib/format.ts).

todos.text: Create logbook actions in tools/ha-history.ts (or separate file)
Implement timeline formatting
Test with dev VM logbook data

items.title: ha_stats — Long-Term Statistics Tool
items.description: Query long-term statistics database for sensor data (hourly/daily/monthly aggregates). WebSocket: `recorder/statistics_during_period`. Actions: get (statistics), list (entities with stats available).
items.body:
## API

WebSocket: `recorder/statistics_during_period`

## Parameters

- entity_id (single or list), start/end, period (5minute/hour/day/week/month), stat_types (mean/min/max/sum/state/change)

## Actions

- `get` — Get statistics for entities over a period
- `list` — List entities that have long-term statistics available

## Use Cases

- "Average energy usage per day this month?"
- "Hourly temperature trends for the past week"
- "Water usage last month vs this month?"

todos.text: Create tools/ha-stats.ts with get and list actions
Use WebSocket recorder/statistics_during_period
Format output with period-appropriate summaries

items.title: ha_events — Live Event Listener Tool
items.description: Subscribe to the HA event bus via WebSocket, capture events during a window, then query/filter results. Designed for the troubleshooting loop: start listening, do the thing, see what happened.
items.body:
## API

WebSocket: `subscribe_events` (optionally filtered by event_type)

## Actions

- `listen` — Start capturing events (event_type filter, duration in seconds, max_count)
- `stop` — Stop active listener early
- `get` — Return captured events with filters (entity_id, search)
- `types` — List event types that fired recently

## Workflow — Troubleshooting Loop

1. `listen event_type=state_changed duration=30` — starts capturing
2. User performs action (press button, trigger sensor)
3. Events captured in memory with timestamps
4. Auto-stops after duration/max_events
5. `get entity_id=switch.problem_switch` — filter to what matters

## Output

Summarize then detail:
```
Captured 47 events in 30s:
  38 state_changed, 6 call_service, 3 automation_triggered

Filtered to switch.problem_switch (3 events):
  10:32:01  state_changed  off → on
  10:32:01  call_service   switch.turn_on
  10:32:15  state_changed  on → off
```

## Implementation Notes

- Background WebSocket subscription buffers events in memory
- Only one active listener at a time
- Session data ephemeral — cleared on next `listen`

todos.text: Create tools/ha-events.ts with listen/stop/get/types actions
Implement WebSocket subscribe_events with background buffer
Add event type filtering and entity post-filtering
Implement summary + detail output formatting
Test troubleshooting loop workflow on dev VM
